Investigators later recovered Alexander's cell phone, but the suspect remained MIA until a nationwide warrant was issued for his arrest. Six days after the brutal assault, officers in Los Angeles, California, nearly 1,000 miles away from the crime scene, said they found the suspect's car abandoned in a parking lot, the same black BMW we saw earlier. Just one day later, after a week-long manhunt, the suspect was located and taken into custody. Alexander was charged with first-degree murder, first-degree attempted murder, and felony harassment. At last check, the 51-year-old is expected back in court this winter.
